name: LUX B&W HC
description: The high-contrast monochrome look needs little introduction. This is a no-nonsense recipe built on Nikon’s Deep Tone Monochrome profile, refined with a custom tone curve that amplifies the contrast. One key advantage is that it works seamlessly in the Zf’s native B&W switch/mode, something I consider a big advantage, even if I don’t shoot black and white all that often. I usually turn off Active D-Lighting when using this recipe. Disabling it helps preserve deep shadows and maximizes the high-contrast look.
author: Mihai Serban
